question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that EntityStakeCalculatorImpl.calculate() is also triggered on ApplicationReadyEvent (calculateOnApplicationReady()), meaning that if the importer restarts while a pending_reward is inflated, the calculation runs again and may compound the inflation if entity_state_start is rebuilt with stale data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lockFromConcurrentUpdates() using 'lock table entity_stake in share row exclusive mode nowait', where if the lock fails (NOWAIT), the entire calculate() transaction is rolled back but the running AtomicBoolean is still reset to false, allowing a retry that may use stale entity_state_start data and produce incorrect pending_reward values?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the entity_state_start snapshot mechanism in createEntityStateStart(), where the snapshot includes entities from entity_history with timestamp_range @> endPeriodTimestamp, and by timing a staking configuration change to overlap with endPeriodTimestamp, cause their account to appear twice in entity_state_start with different staked_node_id values, inflating staked_to_me for a target account?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the pending_reward in entity_stake is updated via 'on conflict (id) do update' in updateEntityStake(), and if the entity_stake_temp table contains a row with a lower pending_reward than the current value (due to a calculation error), the conflict resolution will overwrite the correct value with an incorrect one, stealing rewards from the account owner?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the REST API pending_reward display logic (accounts.js) uses a CASE expression that returns 0 when decline_reward is true, but the underlying entity_stake.pending_reward is not zeroed out, so if an attacker can toggle decline_reward off just before claiming, they can collect rewards that were accumulated while decline_reward appeared true to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the ending_period CTE in updateEntityStake() falls back to the entity's creation timestamp when no entity_stake row exists, and by creating an account with a manipulated creation timestamp that predates all node_stake records, cause the ending_period to select an epoch_day with a very high reward_rate, inflating their initial pending_reward?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the forfeited_period CTE uses 'distinct on(node_id) order by node_id, consensus_timestamp' (ascending), selecting the earliest node_stake for the forfeited epoch_day, while the reward calculation uses the latest node_stake for the ending period, creating an asymmetry that under-deducts the forfeited reward and inflates pending_reward?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the proxy_staking CTE in updateEntityStake(), which sums balance from entity_state_start for all accounts with staked_account_id pointing to them, by creating a large number of zero-balance accounts that stake to their account, causing the staked_to_me calculation to include these accounts and inflate stake_total_start?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that onStakingRewardTransfer() in SqlEntityListener only updates stake_period_start if current.getStakePeriodStart() < stakePeriodStart, and by having a concurrent entity update transaction set stake_period_start to a future value in the same ParserContext, prevent the reward payout from resetting stake_period_start, allowing the account to earn rewards for the same period again?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the balance_snapshot CTE in createEntityStateStart() uses 'distinct on (account_id) order by account_id, consensus_timestamp desc', selecting the most recent balance snapshot, and by submitting a transaction that creates an account_balance entry with an inflated balance just before the snapshot window closes, cause their stake_total_start to be over-counted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the entity_state_start table is truncated at the start of each createEntityStateStart() call (CLEANUP_TABLE_SQL), and if a concurrent calculate() call reads entity_state_start between the truncate and the re-population, it will see an empty table and compute pending_reward as 0 for all accounts, effectively stealing all pending rewards?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the MIGRATION_SQL in RecalculatePendingRewardMigration uses 'epoch_day <= (select end_stake_period from entity_stake where id = 800)' to bound the reward_rate CTE, and if the entity_stake row for account 800 has an incorrect end_stake_period (e.g., set too high), the migration will include future reward rates in the pending_reward calculation, inflating rewards?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the pending_reward calculation in updateEntityStake() uses stake_total_start_whole_bar from the previous period's entity_stake (ending_period_stake_state), not the current period's entity_state_start, and by timing a large deposit before the previous period's snapshot and a withdrawal before the current period's snapshot, maintain a high stake_total_start_whole_bar while having a low actual balance?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the entity_stake_history insert in updateEntityStake() uses 'int8range(lower(e.timestamp_range), lower(t.timestamp_range))' to set the history timestamp_range, and if lower(t.timestamp_range) equals lower(e.timestamp_range), an empty range is created, causing the history row to be invisible to range queries and preventing audit of reward changes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the updated() query in EntityStakeRepository checks if the last epoch_day in node_stake equals the end_stake_period of account 0.0.800's entity_stake, and if no node_stake rows exist (epoch_day = -1), updated() returns true and calculate() skips all pending_reward updates, leaving stale inflated values?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the ending_period CTE in updateEntityStake() uses 'order by consensus_timestamp limit 1' to select the earliest unprocessed staking period, and if multiple node_stake rows exist for the same epoch_day with different consensus_timestamps, the wrong row is selected, causing the reward_rate join to use an incorrect rate and inflate pending_reward?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the stake_total_start in entity_stake_temp is computed as 'ess.balance + coalesce(ps.staked_to_me, 0)' from entity_state_start, and if the balance in entity_state_start is computed incorrectly due to a missing account_balance snapshot (coalesce to 0) plus a large positive balance_change, the stake_total_start is inflated?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the RecalculatePendingRewardMigration only runs for MAINNET and TESTNET (FIRST_NONZERO_REWARD_RATE_TIMESTAMP map), and if the network is configured as a custom network, the migration is skipped entirely, leaving incorrect pending_reward values from the original buggy calculation permanently in entity_stake?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the pending_reward case expression in updateEntityStake() uses 'when reward_rate is null then pending_reward' (preserving the old value) when no node_stake info exists for the staked node, and by staking to a node that has no node_stake record, cause their pending_reward to accumulate indefinitely without being reset?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the entity_state_start snapshot includes entities from entity_history (historical records) as well as the current entity table, and by having a historical record with staked_account_id pointing to their account that overlaps with endPeriodTimestamp, cause their staked_to_me to be double-counted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the getEndPeriodTimestamp() in EntityStakeRepositoryCustomImpl uses a raw JDBC query with positional parameter '?' but also references '$1' in the subquery (a PostgreSQL-style parameter), creating a parameter binding mismatch that could cause the wrong stakingRewardAccount ID to be used, selecting an incorrect ending period timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the pending_reward in entity_stake is a long (bigint) and the calculation 'reward_rate * stake_total_start_whole_bar' can overflow for large stake amounts and high reward rates, causing the pending_reward to wrap to a negative or very small value, effectively stealing rewards from high-balance stakers?
